behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source collectors -> report assembler -> summary input payload. A unit test of one function is too narrow because the bug appears when wiring multiple source adapters together. Real OS/network is not required to prove this behavior; in-process fakes for the three source clients plus an assertion on the assembled payload are sufficient. Higher-level rejection: System was considered, but behavior text focuses on "content passed" (data composition), not real HTTP transport behavior. Adversarial critique: if each source has strict real API response-shape quirks only seen over real calls, System might be needed.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestration modules with three fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields. Execute collection + assembly, intercept the exact object/string sent to summary-generation, and assert all three source sections are present with correct provenance and no source dropped/overwritten.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is cross-module resilience logic: one source failure must propagate as section-level fallback while pipeline continues. This is beyond a single function but does not require real network outage simulation; a source adapter fake raising timeout/connection error exercises the real fallback path. Higher-level rejection: System was considered, but behavior text requires graceful degradation semantics, not OS/network timing fidelity. Adversarial critique: if production failure only occurs due to real client-library exception classes or socket-level behavior, System could be required.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with two sources returning valid data and one source adapter throwing the same exception type used in production handling. Assert process does not abort, output contains a `data unavailable` marker only for the failing section, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality/fidelity, not just wrapper logic. A stubbed generator would always pass and cannot reveal hallucination, omission, or value distortion. This matches LLM-DEP directly: repeated real model runs can vary, and truthfulness to input values must be validated against real generated text. Higher-level rejection: Workflow was considered, but behavior text covers one generation decision (single summary step), not multiple agentic stages. Adversarial critique: if summary is deterministic template code (no AI/ML judgment), Integration would be sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Provide representative input fixtures with known key metrics and issue/deploy highlights, call the real summary model, and score output for factual grounding (all required key values present, no contradictory numbers/status). Use repeated runs with acceptance thresholds to handle nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes external email delivery and inbox receipt, which requires real network/external API behavior. In-process mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, payload encoding, provider acceptance, or delivery semantics. Agentic is not the minimum here because the assertion is end-to-end transport/delivery correctness; summary text can be deterministic for this test. Adversarial critique: if organization treats summary-generation quality as part of "correctly," Agentic could be required in addition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in an environment using real source endpoints (or staging equivalents) and real email delivery path to a test inbox. Verify the inbox receives one message for configured recipients and that subject/body/attachments contain expected report sections end-to-end.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is state handoff across modules/runs: delivery failure -> local persistence -> subsequent retry logic. This can be reproduced with a failing email client fake and real local temp filesystem without real network/provider. System was considered because "service unreachable" sounds networked, but behavior text is about fallback and retry state transitions, which deterministic failure injection can cover. Adversarial critique: if retry depends on real scheduler/cron process timing or provider-specific transient errors, System may be necessary.
behaviors.test_description: First run: inject email client failure, assert report is persisted to local file/queue. Second run: make email client succeed, assert persisted report is retried and sent, then cleared/marked delivered so it is not resent again.
